title: chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. atratum
intro: Chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. atratum, also known as the Corn Marigold, is a flowering plant in the daisy family native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to improve digestion and reduce fever. The Corn Marigold is also valued for its health benefits, including its potential to reduce the risk of certain diseases, such as cancer and cardiovascular disease, due to its high content of antioxidants and flavonoids. Chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. atratum can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
